sections.paragraph:
f. (from the root גָּלַל see the etym. note.)-

(1) fountain, spring, i.q. גַּל No. 2. Plur. Jos 15:19 Jud 1:15.

(2) a bowl, reservoir, so called from its roundness; used of the bowl or oil-vessel of the holy candlestick, Zec 4:3 comp. 2, where there is in masc. גֹּל. Ecc 12:6, in describing old age and death, עַד־אֲשֶׁר לֹא־יֵרָתֵק חֶבֶל הַכֶּםֶף וְתָרֻץ גֻּלַּת הַזָּהָב “before the silver cord be severed, and the golden lamp be broken.”

(3) a ball, a small globe, on the capital of columns, 1Ki 7:41 2Ch 4:12, 13 2Ch 4:13.
